name: Electric Park
description: Electric Park is a genre of defunct amusement parks operating from the 1880s to the 1920s, to showcase the electrification of, and the advent of electricity as a utility in, the United States.  Many were constructed as trolley parks and owned by electric companies and streetcar companies.  After 1903, the success of Coney Island inspired a proliferation of parks named Luna Park and Electric Park, and the World's Columbian Exposition of 1893 inspired the formation of White City amusement parks at about the same time.
